questions.question: Which CSS property must be set on the scroll container for scroll snap to work?
questions.explanation: The container must have overflow set to auto or scroll to enable scrolling. Without it, scroll snap has no effect.

questions.question: What does 'scroll-snap-type: x mandatory' do?
options: On the horizontal axis, always snap to the nearest snap position
On the vertical axis, snap when near a position
questions.explanation: x mandatory means: snap on the horizontal axis, and always snap to the nearest position when the user stops scrolling.

questions.question: What is the difference between 'mandatory' and 'proximity' strictness?
options: mandatory always snaps, proximity snaps only when near a snap position
mandatory snaps faster, proximity snaps slower
mandatory requires JavaScript, proximity works without JS
mandatory is for mobile, proximity is for desktop
questions.explanation: mandatory forces the scroll container to always land on a snap position. proximity only snaps when the scroll position is close enough to a snap point.

questions.question: Which property on child elements determines which edge aligns to the snap position?
questions.explanation: scroll-snap-align on child elements accepts start, center, or end values to specify which edge aligns to the snap position.

questions.question: What does 'scroll-padding' do in a scroll snap container?
options: Adds padding inside the container that offsets the snap position
questions.explanation: scroll-padding defines offsets inside the scroll container that affect where snap positions are calculated. Useful for avoiding fixed headers.

questions.question: Which of the following is a valid combination for scroll-snap-type?
questions.explanation: Valid combinations pair an axis (x, y, block, inline, both) with a strictness keyword (mandatory, proximity). x mandatory is a valid example.

questions.question: What does 'scroll-snap-align: center' do?
options: Centers the child element within the snap position
Snaps the center of the child to the snap position
questions.explanation: scroll-snap-align: center means the center of the child element aligns with the snap position of the scroll container.

questions.question: Which property prevents users from scrolling past a snap item?
questions.explanation: scroll-snap-stop: always forces the scroll container to stop at every snap position, preventing users from scrolling past items.

questions.question: What is the main accessibility concern with scroll-snap-type: mandatory?
options: It can trap keyboard focus if content changes dynamically
It prevents screen readers from reading content
questions.explanation: mandatory snapping can trap users if content changes dynamically. If a new item is added, the user might be forced to a new position unexpectedly.

questions.question: How do you create a horizontal carousel where each item snaps to the start of the container?
options: Container: scroll-snap-type: x mandatory; Children: scroll-snap-align: start
Container: scroll-snap-type: y mandatory; Children: scroll-snap-align: start
Container: overflow: hidden; Children: display: flex
Container: scroll-snap-align: start; Children: scroll-snap-type: x mandatory
questions.explanation: x mandatory on the container with scroll-snap-align: start on children creates a horizontal carousel where each item snaps to the container's left/start edge.

questions.question: How do you account for a fixed 60px header in a scroll snap layout?
questions.explanation: scroll-padding-block-start: 60px offsets the snap position by 60px from the top, so content snaps below the fixed header.

questions.question: What does scroll-margin do in scroll snap?
options: Adds margin to the child that offsets its snap position
Creates visual gaps between snapped items
questions.explanation: scroll-margin on child elements creates a margin outside them that affects where their snap position is calculated, useful for creating gaps between items.

questions.question: Which browser supports CSS Scroll Snap without prefixes?
questions.explanation: Firefox 68+, Chrome 69+, Safari 11+, and Edge 79+ all support modern CSS Scroll Snap. IE does not support it.

questions.question: What happens if you set scroll-snap-type but forget overflow on the container?
options: Scroll snap won't work because the container doesn't scroll
It works the same, scroll snap adds overflow automatically
Only snapping works but scrolling is disabled
The browser throws a CSS error
questions.explanation: Without overflow-x: auto or overflow-y: auto (or scroll), the container can't scroll and scroll snap has no visible effect.

questions.question: What is the effect of scroll-snap-stop: always on a specific child?
options: The container must stop at that child; it can't scroll past it
That child doesn't participate in snapping
That child has a stop animation
The child is hidden when not snapped
questions.explanation: scroll-snap-stop: always ensures the scroll container can't skip past that particular child. It forces a stop at each snap position.

questions.question: Can you use CSS Scroll Snap with CSS Grid?
options: Yes, use grid-auto-flow: column and overflow-x: auto on the grid container
No, scroll snap only works with flexbox
Yes, but only for vertical scrolling
questions.explanation: CSS Grid works with scroll snap. Use grid-auto-flow: column for horizontal scrolling grids, and set scroll-snap-type on the grid container.

questions.question: What touch-specific CSS property helps smooth scrolling on iOS?
questions.explanation: -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch enables momentum scrolling on iOS, which improves the feel of scroll snap on touch devices.

questions.question: What's the recommended strictness for content browsing experiences like image galleries?
questions.explanation: proximity is recommended for content browsing because it allows users to stop mid-scroll if they want, while still providing snap assistance.

questions.question: What should you combine with scroll snap for a polished, app-like feel?
questions.explanation: scroll-behavior: smooth makes the scroll transition between snap positions animated rather than instant, creating a polished feel.

questions.question: Which ARIA role is appropriate for a scroll snap carousel?
questions.explanation: role="region" with a descriptive aria-label helps screen reader users understand the purpose of the scroll snap carousel.
